_publications._publicationTitle: Modeling Tumor: Lymphatic Interactions in Lymphatic Metastasis of Triple Negative Breast Cancer

_contextSnippet: TAME chambers feature two configurations: separate wells enabling monoculture spheroid growth and linked wells enabling parallel coculture where cancer spheroids and lymphatic networks develop in adjacent connected compartments, facilitating spatial separation with paracrine communication
details: TAME microfluidic devices have two configurations: (1) separate wells for independent monoculture spheroid formation; (2) linked wells for parallel coculture where two cell populations develop in adjacent compartments connected to allow secretome sharing without direct cell contact; originally used to model TNBC and lymphatic endothelial cell interactions over extended culture periods
